EX 3 3 – RF Picking for Active Currently Active p icking occurs in either Dematic or via Pick Sheet. To phase out Dematic and bring that side of Active picking into the WMOS fold, EX33 is customize d the Make Pick Cart and Pack Pick Cart transactions to fit McLane’s needs. McLane sells “Single Sell , ” “Repack , ” and other smaller items which are slotted in WMOS Active Pick Locations. These items are picked into physical totes that act as an oLPN , typically containing many items in one tote. Picking in these areas occurs either in Dematic Pick to Light or by Pick Sheet . To sunset these processes, McLane is utilizing WMOS to execute picking . For conveyable sites, an oLPN X-REF is required and scanned from the top of a physical tote during the picking process. WMOS is customized to add a user level configuration for the total number of totes a user can work on at a time. Only a certain number of oLPN X-REF scans will be possible based on this configured value. WMOS is customized to add an X-REF oLPN prompt at the beginning of the Pack Pick Cart transaction . This scan associate s the WMOS generated oLPNs with the physically scanned X-REF on the tote. Aerial scanners in other systems view this X-REF on the tote, and communicate with WMOS based on this X-REF. When this X-REF is utilized as a scan, it is displayed to the user during the transaction in place of oLPN number. Additionally, McLane requires changes in behavior of the RF Pack Pick Cart transaction to increase the end user’s throughput when picking and facilitate ease of use . For this reason, WMOS is customized to auto-populate the expected quantity at the quantity prompt , instead of the user hunting for the right keys. Further, WMOS adds a custom control key to prompt for the location instead of the item barcode if necessary. Lastly , the display s of screens throughout the transaction are customized to meet McLane’s requests. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF N Reporting N Other N McLane grocery picks “Single Sell , ” “Repack , ” and other smaller items in WMOS Active Locations. These items are picked directly into totes which function as the outbound container. Each container has its own oLPN Shipping Label . These Active oLPNs contain multiple items picked from the same areas. Some of these areas is a race-track style pick module. For race-track picking, WMOS enhances task pickup and detail sortation logic to begin the next picking task in the closest possible physical location to where the picker has ended their previous task. WMOS is customized to do an additional user level check to allow a number of oLPNs a user can work with at a time. At the beginning of an RF Pack Pick Cart task, WMOS prints all oLPN Shipping Labels for oLPNs on the task through base . After the oLPN /Size Type Screen, which displays the total count of oLPNs which the user should pick up, they are immediately brought to the first Pick Location and its associated Item Barcode scan. At the Item Barcode scan , WMOS performs all base item validations and other validations to ensure that the product scanned is correct. A custom control key is added to give the user the ability to scan the Location Barcode instead of the Item Barcode is unusable. The user can execute a complete short at this point. After the Item Barcode or Location is scanned , WMOS is enhanced to auto-populate the expected aggregate To Be Picked quantity from this Pick Location in the quantity prompt. At this point, the user can partially short the expected quantity from the Pick Location. I f the user proceeds past the quantity prompt and has not completely shorted, they are presented with the oLPN Confirmation scan(s). At this point in the Race-track flow, the user scans the “GLP” (McLane Terminology ) , which is a barcode on top of the physical container. WMOS updates this value as the oLPNs Cross Reference field and from this point on, is interchangeable with the TC LPN ID. WMOS is enhanced to not prompt for the oLPN confirmation scan if the quantity picked from a Pick Location is being packed entirely into one oLPN , unless the user is in a race-track flow and the oLPN has not yet been assigned a GLP. In this event, the user is prompted for the oLPN scan regardless, to assign the GLP to the oLPN . Assumptions This extension modifies the Pack Pick Cart transaction only . Base configuration for shortage reason code is configured to default without prompting the user. Cigarette Picking does not occur through Pack Pick Cart . Chase for Active Items picked with Pack Pick Cart occurs at the oLPN level . This means oLPNs waiting on chased items are set aside at a designated area/location to receive their chase picks and then are packed and move d to loading. RF Pickers have hip printers to print oLPN shipping label during picking. Base Task Group Validations occur when Pack Pick Cart is entered through tasking. Alternate pick location capabilities are not used by McLane in RF Pack Pick Cart flows and are not in scope of this extension. For EX33 RF Pack Pick Cart Transaction Parameters assume the following configuration : Pick carts are oLPN carts only. Tote carts are a different flow and would require additional scope . 1-1 Validation oLPN ? = Prompt for LPN after Item is Picked This configuration results in WMOS prompting for oLPN /X-REF confirmation in an event where a picked quantity is being distributed across multiple oLPNs but does not prompt for oLPN /X-REF confirmation if the picked quantity is going into a single oLPN . 4-4 Active Vérification Mode = ‘ Scan Item Enter Quantity ’ (OR) ‘Enter Quantity ’ Quantity prompt he re is pre-populated with the expected value per McLane requirement. Flows requiring transaction level barcode scan will be set with ‘Scan Item Enter Quantity’, flows that only need to prompt barcode scan when the item level enablement is set will be set to ‘Enter Quantity’. 5-5 Zone Picking = 0 1 6- 1 6 Prompt for Item Attributes = Do not display item property entry 17-17 Display oLPN Type/Size = Yes This displays total totes needed for task 20-20 Display Slot Number = Yes 21-21 Prompt for Reason Code = No 23-23 Default Reason Code = Configured to Valid Value 24-24 Prompt for Pick Cart Number – Tasking Mode = No 26-26 Aggregate Location Picks for Item = No 59-59 Enable oLPN Cross Reference Support = No 61-61 Batch Print oLPN label = Beginning of oLPN Pick Cart Task WMOS prints oLPN labels in slot assignment order ascending: 1, 2, 3 Users apply printed Shipping Labels to totes in the order that they are printed and in the same order each time via SOP. SOP dictates that they are applied in ascending slot order , the same way they are printed. Reprinting o L PN Shipping Labels occurs through standard base methods. No additional custom printing is in scope for this extension. McLane pick locations are not dynamic. Tasks are specified to maintain the ‘ Race-track ’ behavior or not. Tasks generated within a Race-track style module should have this behavior and tasks generated outside of a Race-track style module should not have this behavior. Tasks are created in Task Creation Rules to proceed along Pick Location Sequence ( LOCN_HDR.LOCN_PIQ_SEQ ) through base configuration. EX35 is invoked upon CTRL -D functionality if enabled. WMOS is enhanced to remove oLPN confirmation screen upon 518 - 518 being configured. User is aware of the inventory being pulled and where it needs to be placed. XREF_OLPN ( GLP Label) will always be scanned, XREF_OLPN will never be null. T here will not be more than 6 totes at a time. L ines 1-4 are statics and hitting enter goes to the next location upon confirming all puts . Hitting enter on oLPN confirmations screen go es to the next screen. MCLN will handle user level configuration for x number of totes a user can be assigned at a time. Single - oLPN tasks are generated to support user level configuration changes. Zone Picking – Pick and Pass anchor task is not created unless all zones have been picked for oLPN . Dependencies Code Dependencies EX35 – RF User Short Auto-A dj ust Inventory Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Process Flow Diagram P ull Packing Out Single oLPN Pull Packing Out Multiple oLPN s Process Steps User initiates work in the appropriate task group by requesting work from WMOS through CTRL -S in the RF main menu. If the packing task assigned is not an EX33 packing task, then base process continues, otherwise WMOS proceeds with EX33 custom processing. WMOS is customized to do a user level check for the total number of totes allowed to be worked with at a time. Base oLPN Size /Type screen is displayed, directing the user how many totes are needed for the task. Custom LPN.XREF_OLPN prompt displays to scan cross reference. This prompt repeats until all oLPNs on the task have a cross reference populated. Base oLPN Shipping Labels are printed in slot order ascending. WMOS directs the user to the Pick Location and prompts to confirm Item Barcode. If user takes exception control key for Location prompt, WMOS prompts the user for Location Barcode scan . Upon confirming Location , WMOS proceeds to total quantity prompt. The user is able to completely short the task detail through base processing at this point through control key. If this process is taken, the user accepts the short message and be brought back to step 3 for a new task detail or the task ends. Otherwise, user scans Item Barcode to confirm and WMOS proceeds to the total quantity prompt. At the total quantity prompt, the user’s input is pre-populated through WMOS custom behavior. The user is able to enter a partial quantity here to partially short the task detail. If this process is taken, they are returned to the quantity prompt, now prompting for the remaining quantity, and execute short control key to short the remaining picks. If a partial quantity is packed, the user proceed s to step 7 and is prompted for oLPN confirmation. If the user packs out the entire quantity, the user proceeds to step 7 and is prompted for oLPN confirmation. WMOS prompts for user for oLPN confirmation. If quantity picked from location is being packed into a single oLPN , WMOS is customized to not prompt for oLPN confirmation screen, and the user is sent back to step 3 for the next task detail or the task ends. If a quantity picked from the location is being packed into multiple oLPNs : The user is prompted to confirm the oLPN or GLP via pressing enter on the auto-populated qty tote number. The oLPN confirmation screen gives oLPN or LPN.XREF_OLPN information if it is populated and shows the qty needed in each tote prompt with the expected tote highlighted and already in the user input field. If oLPN /GLP is confirmed, the user returns to step 3 for a new task detail or the task ends. Else, total number of totes is more than 3, user hits enter to confirm first 3 puts into first 3 totes and is taken to the next screen for the next 3 totes to co nfirm puts. Upon all puts being confirmed WM then makes all expected updates. Enablement In addition to the validations stated in 6.1.1 , this section requires further validation to execute behavior. The custom RF transaction parameter 519 - 519 is set to “Y”, otherwise the custom functionality detailed in section 6.1.2 is disabled. The custom User Profile Configuration edit box is added and configured from bytes 1-99 with a numeric edit box, otherwise WMOS takes the value as ‘NULL ’ and the custom functionality detailed in section 6.1.2 is disabled. MHE No changes to base behavior for PIX generation as part of this extension. RF RF Pack Pick Cart Program Platform Available Text Based (RF Gun) Y UI (RF UI) Y Mobile (DM) N Base RF Pack Pick Cart transaction is enhanced with display changes and pre-population of total quantity values. This transaction is also enhanced to sort task details of a new task to minimize physical movement. Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing occur s . Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX33 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX33 is turned off, and no custom EX33 processing occur s . Lastly, the Custom RF transaction parameter must be enabled. If base transaction parameter for Active Verification Mode is set to ‘2’ – ‘Scan Item, Enter Quantity’ and custom transaction parameter 516 - 516 is set to “Y”, then the custom functionality within this section is enabled. If custom transaction parameter 516 - 516 is set to “N”, or not set up at all, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base processing. User Level oLPN Assignment WMOS is customized to add additional parameter configuration on User Profile Configuration screen. WMOS takes this number and assigns the user a total number of oLPNs /Tasks to begin picking. WMOS assigns the highest-level priority task to the user and merges (N-1) additional single oLPN tasks of the same task type (Where N is the setting on the user for the max tasks per user) from the select task sequencing into a “master task” which is now a multi- oLPN pick cart task. Enablement In addition to the validations stated in 6.1.1 , this section requires further validation to execute behavior. The custom RF transaction parameter 519 - 519 is set to “Y”, otherwise the custom functionality detailed in section 6.1.2 is disabled. The custom User Profile Configuration edit box is added and configured from bytes 1-99 with a numeric edit box, otherwise WMOS takes the value as ‘NULL ’ and the custom functionality detailed in section 6.1.2 is disabled. Validations Upon invoking CTRL - S functionality for task assignment mode, WMOS checks parameter 519 - 519 = ‘Y ’ if true move forward in custom logic. * The user does not have to literally key CTRL -S, but this behavior is not enabled if the user has keyed CTRL -E to enter the task. * WMOS then does user level check to validate the number of oLPNs allotted for the user to perform at a time. Navigation N/A Updates N/A Screen 1 - Zone Picking – Pick and Pass WMOS is customized to add additional parameter configuration to utilize Zone Picking – Pick and Pass workflow on EX33. Validations After loading the task and performing base validations, WMOS checks if the task was entered via CTRL -S behavior. Updates Upon loading the task, if enablement and validations pass and the task is for multiple details, then perform the following logic to re-sequence the task details . This behavior is only applicable when the user is involved in a sequential task pickup through the CTRL -S flow: 1 – completes a task, 2 – CTRL -S assigns another task. Once a user completes a task in the CTRL -S workflow , the completed task’s Last Visited Pick Location (TASK_DTL.PICK _SEQ_CODE) is stored in memory as CTRL -S behavior brings them into the next task. If this is the FIRST task that a user is assigned through CTRL -S, there will be no task held in the memory and there will be no race-track behavior enabled. This record gives the TASK_DTL.PICK_SEQ_CODE of the most recently modified record After this record is determined, WMOS finds the next logical task detail in the task being loaded to proceed down the pick path. This detail from the new task is the first task detail displayed to the user, instead of task detail 1. The user proceeds through the task details in sequential order after starting at the determined closest task detail while still proceeding forward down the pick path. After the user completes the task details which were past the point of entry, the user is looped back to the beginning of the task to finish picking all of the task details. Item Flag to Force Barcode Scan Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing occurs. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX33 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX33 is turned off, and no custom EX33 processing occurs. Lastly, the Custom RF transaction parameter must be enabled. If base transaction parameter for Active Verification Mode is set to ‘2’ – ‘Scan Item, Enter Quantity’ and custom transaction parameter 516-516 is set to “Y”, then the custom functionality within this section is enabled. If custom transaction parameter 516-516 is set to “N”, or not set up at all, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base processing. If the Pack Pick Cart transaction is configured to only prompt for quantity where transaction parameter 4-4 is = ‘1’ , there will still cases where McLane desires the Item Barcode scan to be forced . Certain items are more expensive and need to be confirmed. For this reason, WMOS is customized to force the Item Barcode scan if the item scanned has a ITEM_CBO.REF_NUM5 populated with ‘1’. This field denotes whether an item is deemed important enough to force the Item Barcode scan even if they transaction itself is not configured to prompt for barcode. Display There are no changes to display in this section of the extension. The user is brought to Screen 2 – Item Prompt when transaction parameter 4-4 = 1. This section is meant to continue custom screen flow, updates and validation even when parm 4-4 = 1. Upon 4-4=1 we still consider item barcode screen if item is found with ITEM_CBO.REF_NUM5 = 1. Else, we do custom processing while skipping screen 2 item prompt screen. CTRL Keys There are no changes to base control keys in this section. Master Task Recovery Upon Exiting a Task McLane faces challenges with users who accidentally exit out of tasks for several reasons (i.e., Network outages, breaks, accidentally hitting CTRL-X). WMOS is enhanced to limit the number of instances this occurs by adding a custom error message CUST/3308 “Exiting Task, continue?” to warn users that they are about to exit a task. Upon instances where WM is unable to display this error message or chose to still exit the task , WM is enhanced to remove this task from task selection criteria (CTRL-S Automatic task assignment mode ) and forces users to enter the task via CTRL-E. This limits the Master task from forming into a new Master task and allows the user to finish the work from where they previously left off. Users are prevented from utilizing CTRL-S flow if currently assigned to a ‘Master’ Task and are thrown custom error message CUST/3309 “Open Master task – Enter Task ID ”. To phase out Dematic and bring that side of Active picking into the WMOS fold, EX33 is customize d the Make Pick Cart and Pack Pick Cart transactions to fit McLane’s needs. McLane sells “Single Sell , ” “Repack , ” and other smaller items which are slotted in WMOS Active Pick Locations. These items are picked into physical totes that act as an oLPN , typically containing many items in one tote. Picking in these areas occurs either in Dematic Pick to Light or by Pick Sheet . To sunset these processes, McLane is utilizing WMOS to execute picking . For conveyable sites, an oLPN X-REF is required and scanned from the top of a physical tote during the picking process. WMOS is customized to add a user level configuration for the total number of totes a user can work on at a time. Only a certain number of oLPN X-REF scans will be possible based on this configured value. WMOS is customized to add an X-REF oLPN prompt at the beginning of the Pack Pick Cart transaction . This scan associate s the WMOS generated oLPNs with the physically scanned X-REF on the tote. Aerial scanners in other systems view this X-REF on the tote, and communicate with WMOS based on this X-REF. When this X-REF is utilized as a scan, it is displayed to the user during the transaction in place of oLPN number. Additionally, McLane requires changes in behavior of the RF Pack Pick Cart transaction to increase the end user’s throughput when picking and facilitate ease of use . For this reason, WMOS is customized to auto-populate the expected quantity at the quantity prompt , instead of the user hunting for the right keys. Further, WMOS adds a custom control key to prompt for the location instead of the item barcode if necessary. Lastly , the display s of screens throughout the transaction are customized to meet McLane’s requests. For race-track picking, WMOS enhances task pickup and detail sortation logic to begin the next picking task in the closest possible physical location to where the picker has ended their previous task. WMOS is customized to do an additional user level check to allow a number of oLPNs a user can work with at a time. At the beginning of an RF Pack Pick Cart task, WMOS prints all oLPN Shipping Labels for oLPNs on the task through base . After the oLPN /Size Type Screen, which displays the total count of oLPNs which the user should pick up, they are immediately brought to the first Pick Location and its associated Item Barcode scan. At the Item Barcode scan , WMOS performs all base item validations and other validations to ensure that the product scanned is correct. A custom control key is added to give the user the ability to scan the Location Barcode instead of the Item Barcode is unusable. The user can execute a complete short at this point. After the Item Barcode or Location is scanned , WMOS is enhanced to auto-populate the expected aggregate To Be Picked quantity from this Pick Location in the quantity prompt. At this point, the user can partially short the expected quantity from the Pick Location. I f the user proceeds past the quantity prompt and has not completely shorted, they are presented with the oLPN Confirmation scan(s). At this point in the Race-track flow, the user scans the “GLP” (McLane Terminology ) , which is a barcode on top of the physical container. WMOS updates this value as the oLPNs Cross Reference field and from this point on, is interchangeable with the TC LPN ID. WMOS is enhanced to not prompt for the oLPN confirmation scan if the quantity picked from a Pick Location is being packed entirely into one oLPN , unless the user is in a race-track flow and the oLPN has not yet been assigned a GLP. In this event, the user is prompted for the oLPN scan regardless, to assign the GLP to the oLPN . Assumptions This extension modifies the Pack Pick Cart transaction only . Base configuration for shortage reason code is configured to default without prompting the user. Cigarette Picking does not occur through Pack Pick Cart . Chase for Active Items picked with Pack Pick Cart occurs at the oLPN level . This means oLPNs waiting on chased items are set aside at a designated area/location to receive their chase picks and then are packed and move d to loading. RF Pickers have hip printers to print oLPN shipping label during picking.

Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing occur s . Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX33 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX33 is turned off, and no custom EX33 processing occur s . Lastly, the Custom RF transaction parameter must be enabled. If base transaction parameter for Active Verification Mode is set to ‘2’ – ‘Scan Item, Enter Quantity’ and custom transaction parameter 516 - 516 is set to “Y”, then the custom functionality within this section is enabled. If custom transaction parameter 516 - 516 is set to “N”, or not set up at all, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base processing. WMOS takes this number and assigns the user a total number of oLPNs /Tasks to begin picking. Validations After loading the task and performing base validations, WMOS checks if the task was entered via CTRL -S behavior. Updates Upon loading the task, if enablement and validations pass and the task is for multiple details, then perform the following logic to re-sequence the task details . This behavior is only applicable when the user is involved in a sequential task pickup through the CTRL -S flow: 1 – completes a task, 2 – CTRL -S assigns another task. Once a user completes a task in the CTRL -S workflow , the completed task’s Last Visited Pick Location (TASK_DTL.PICK _SEQ_CODE) is stored in memory as CTRL -S behavior brings them into the next task. If this is the FIRST task that a user is assigned through CTRL -S, there will be no task held in the memory and there will be no race-track behavior enabled. This record gives the TASK_DTL.PICK_SEQ_CODE of the most recently modified record After this record is determined, WMOS finds the next logical task detail in the task being loaded to proceed down the pick path. This detail from the new task is the first task detail displayed to the user, instead of task detail 1. The user proceeds through the task details in sequential order after starting at the determined closest task detail while still proceeding forward down the pick path. After the user completes the task details which were past the point of entry, the user is looped back to the beginning of the task to finish picking all of the task details.

Continuous Cluster Flow During EX33 ‘Master’ task creation, users can complete totes/ oLPNs during picking prior to other totes being completed. Upon completion, users will verify the remaining totes still open and add the new tote to the end of the slot assignments, allowing the user to continuously pick totes while going down a pick path. WM b uild new task list of all Tasks of the same Task Type, associated to EXT_SYS_SHIPMENT_ID in 0103, 0104, 0105 (defined by C-E33-SHP = 3 ) . WM f ind s the t ask s within the new list with a Location closest to or in front of the current Location. As a tiebreaker (multiple tasks have the same Location), use Priority (lowest priority number means Task should generally be picked first) . Master Task Recovery Upon Exiting a Task McLane faces challenges with users who accidentally exit out of tasks for several reasons (i.e., Network outages, breaks, accidentally hitting CTRL-X). WMOS is enhanced to limit the number of instances this occurs by adding a custom error message CUST/3308 “Exiting Task, continue?” to warn users that they are about to exit a task.

Upon instances where WM is unable to display this error message or chose to still exit the task , WM is enhanced to remove this task from task selection criteria (CTRL-S Automatic task assignment mode ) and forces users to enter the task via CTRL-E.
